summary: LEGACY backend fully tested end-to-end via public URL. 32/32 pytest tests pass covering health, auth (guest/me/logout), mentors (list/filter/search/detail/reviews/404), categories (15), vault (9 items + collections grouping), careers (8 + detail), community events (6 + kind filter), bookings (mock payment + sessions counter increment), impact stats, AI Career Compass Claude Sonnet 4.5 (non-stream + SSE stream + history), MongoDB _id leak checks, and 401 coverage on all authenticated endpoints. No CORS issues. One intermittent 502 on POST /api/career-chat was observed in the first run (LiteLLM 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0, Max budget: 0.0' error from the Emergent LLM proxy) but disappeared on retry — the AI integration is functionally working (verified via curl with real Claude output). Main agent should be aware of this transient LLM budget error and consider adding a retry/backoff wrapper for robustness.

minor.issue: Intermittent 502 from upstream LiteLLM proxy with 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0, Max budget: 0.0'. Recovered on retry. Consider a single retry/backoff for transient LLM upstream errors and returning a clearer 503 (Service Unavailable) instead of 502 so clients can retry.

On LLM failure the stream yields 'data: [error: ...]' but still terminates with 'data: [DONE]'. Frontend must specifically detect the [error:] prefix and surface it — otherwise it looks like a normal empty response.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Wrap emergentintegrations LlmChat.send_message / stream_message with a single retry on litellm.BadRequestError('Budget has been exceeded') / 5xx before returning 502.
OPTIONAL: On the frontend, when consuming /api/career-chat/stream, detect chunks starting with '[error:' and show a friendly retry UI.

OPTIONAL: Non-streaming replay in /api/career-chat re-sends the full prior history through LlmChat.send_message, which duplicates LLM cost & latency on every message. Consider building an initial history via LlmChat's own memory or skipping replay — the SDK already persists per session_id.
OPTIONAL: Fix pytest deprecation warning — session_id fixture in TestCareerChat should be @classmethod or module-scoped.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 574 lines — approaching the 700-line threshold; consider splitting into routers/ (auth, mentors, vault, careers, community, chat).
Auth uses raw session tokens with no HMAC/signature; acceptable for MVP but flag before prod.
get_current_user returns the entire user document; safe (no _id), but consider a UserOut projection for tighter contracts.
on_event('startup')/('shutdown') are deprecated in FastAPI; migrate to lifespan handlers.
seed_data on every startup uses count==0 gate — idempotent and safe.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data. Backend auto-seeds 6 mentors, 9 vault items, 8 careers, 6 events, 5 reviews on startup as expected. Test-created bookings prefixed with TEST_ in notes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public base URL is EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL in /app/frontend/.env (=https://knowledge-vault-201.preview.emergentagent.com). All 32 backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_legacy_backend.py — reuse them. Guest login (POST /api/auth/guest) is the reliable way to obtain a Bearer token for authenticated tests. LLM chat can be transiently flaky due to Emergent LLM proxy budget errors — one-retry is usually sufficient. Booking test mutates mentor.sessions counter for mnt_ananya; downstream tests that hard-code that value should read it dynamically.
rca_of_issue: First-run failure of test_career_chat_nonstream (502 Bad Gateway). RCA: backend log showed 'litellm.BadRequestError: OpenAIException - Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0, Max budget: 0.0' from the LiteLLM proxy behind the Emergent LLM key. server.py catches this and re-raises as HTTPException 502. Reproduction: bursty parallel calls to /api/career-chat can trigger transient budget errors. Mitigation: retry once with exponential backoff before returning 502; or map budget errors to 429/503 so clients auto-retry. Verified fix path via direct curl — both non-stream and stream endpoints returned real Claude Sonnet 4.5 content after the transient error cleared.
